body.title: [triage] Define the carried intent-artifact accretion contract across front-half levels L1-L4
body.intention: I intend that the intent artifact carried through the front-half levels (interviewer -> intention-extractor -> designer -> ticketer) has an EXPLICIT field-by-field data contract, so each level's 'adds a layer' is a defined accretion onto a shared object rather than an ad-hoc handoff between devices.
body.description:
The workflow-levels map (architecture/cc.0.workflow-levels.*, 2026-07-10) identified the biggest FRONT-half gap: Vetinari (L1/L2) and Hubert (L3/L4) exist as devices, but they are NOT assembled as an intent-ACCRETING assembly line. The napkin framing is 'each level takes the prior output + a knowledge body and ADDS a layer' — but the thing being added-to (the carried intent artifact) has no defined shape. This ticket defines that contract: what fields the intent artifact holds after each of L1-L4, so the accretion is a typed, inspectable object (the compilation seam), not a device-to-device guess.

**Affected files:** design deliverable first — a decision (D-intent-artifact-contract-*) and/or extension of architecture/cc.0.intent.* and architecture/cc.0.workflow-levels.*; then likely unseen_university/devices/vetinari/ + devices/hubert/ to emit/consume the shaped artifact. Discovery in sprint.
**Design rules:** intention = present-tense contract; homogeneity over special-case; every field carries its why; canonical-memory-home.
**Scope boundary:** IN — the field-by-field contract for the carried intent artifact across L1(interviewer)->L2(intention-extractor)->L3(designer)->L4(ticketer), reconciled to the existing Intent Doc (architecture/cc.0.intent.*) and build_packet inputs. OUT — the back-half levels; OUT — the LLM-middle; OUT — wiring the devices to actually pass it (that is a follow-on once the contract is defined).
**Test plan:** design deliverable — proof is the artifact (the defined contract). Honest close: shipped-unproven naming 'design artifact, no red form' UNLESS the contract is realized as a validated schema (then a schema-conformance red->green becomes possible).
**Completion criteria:** a decision/architecture-point defines each field of the carried intent artifact, which level adds it, and its why; reconciled to architecture/cc.0.intent.* and the build_packet.v1 inputs; the OPEN napkin question (is a level a device/workflow/graph-tree node) is NOT pre-decided here (Akien's call).

Related: architecture/cc.0.workflow-levels.*, architecture/cc.0.intent.*, project_staged_intention_compiler_pipeline, project_build_packet_pre_inference_compiler, project_intentions_as_living_entities.
